About Me

Geography graduate &
spatial thinker.

Background

I'm a 1st-class Geography BSc graduate from the University of Manchester, looking for opportunities in GIS-informed consultation or technical roles. My interests span planning, landscape ecology and biodiversity net gain (BNG), and sustainable development — opportunities aligned with these goals are always welcome.

My dissertation — Please Marten the Gap — developed a Python-based spatial prioritisation framework for red squirrel conservation, combining species interaction modelling with multi-criteria decision analysis to guide reserve selection under pine marten recolonisation scenarios in Cumbria. It received a grade of 78.

Outside of GIS, I'm drawn to the intersection of computational methods and real-world conservation: how models can inform better land management decisions, how stakeholder engagement shapes conservation outcomes, and how transparent, open-source tools can build trust between scientists and practitioners.

Education

BSc Geography — 1st Class Honours
University of Manchester
2022 – 2025

I'm open to roles in GIS, conservation technology, spatial planning, and related fields. Get in touch via any of the channels below.
